epic_name: AI Co-Pilot Intelligence — Memory, Skills & Dynamic Context
auth_note: All endpoints require Bearer JWT. Use test user token from E2 auth setup. companyId derived from JWT context.

endpoints.description: Create a new AI memory for the authenticated user (E5b-1 AC-2)

body.content: I prefer Net 30 payment terms for new customers

db_verification.expected: Row exists with category='PREFERENCE', source='EXPLICIT', importance >= 1.0, userId matches, companyId matches

body.content: User frequently opens overdue invoices view

db_verification.query: SELECT importance FROM ai_memories WHERE id = '{{response.data.id}}'

test_cases.name: Create memory — memory disabled returns domain error
Create memory — disabled category returns domain error
test_cases.precondition: User memory settings enabledCategories does not include WORKFLOW

endpoints.description: List memories for authenticated user with optional filters (E5b-1 AC-3)

test_cases.name: List memories — filter by search text
List memories — empty list returns 200 with empty array
List memories — company scoping (user cannot see other company memories)
test_cases.precondition: Memory created under companyA; request as user in companyB

db_verification.expected: Empty array — memories from companyA not visible

test_cases.name: List memories — ordered by importance DESC then recency
test_cases.precondition: Create memories with different importance scores

db_verification.expected: First memory has highest importance score

endpoints.description: Update a memory's content or category (E5b-1 AC-4)

body.content: Updated: I now prefer Net 60 payment terms

db_verification.query: SELECT content, updated_at FROM ai_memories WHERE id = '{{memoryId}}'

test_cases.name: Update memory — ownership enforcement (other user's memory)
test_cases.precondition: Memory belongs to userA; request as userB

endpoints.description: Hard delete a single memory (E5b-1 AC-5)

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_memories WHERE id = '{{memoryId}}'

test_cases.name: Delete memory — ownership enforcement (other user's memory)

endpoints.description: Delete all memories for user in current company (E5b-1 AC-6)

test_cases.name: Forget all — happy path with existing memories
test_cases.precondition: User has 5+ memories in current company

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_memories WHERE user_id = '{{userId}}' AND company_id = '{{companyId}}'

test_cases.name: Forget all — no memories returns deletedCount 0
Forget all — does not delete other company's memories
test_cases.precondition: User has memories in companyA and companyB; request as companyA

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_memories WHERE user_id = '{{userId}}' AND company_id = '{{companyBId}}'

endpoints.description: Get user's memory settings (lazy upsert — creates defaults if none exist) (E5b-1 AC-9)

test_cases.name: Get settings — lazy upsert creates defaults for new user
test_cases.precondition: New user with no ai_memory_settings row

db_verification.query: SELECT is_enabled, retention_days, max_memories FROM ai_memory_settings WHERE user_id = '{{newUserId}}' AND company_id = '{{companyId}}'
db_verification.expected: Row created with isEnabled=true, retentionDays=365, maxMemories=500, all 5 categories enabled

endpoints.description: Update memory settings (E5b-1 AC-9)

db_verification.query: SELECT is_enabled FROM ai_memory_settings WHERE user_id = '{{userId}}' AND company_id = '{{companyId}}'

notes: E5b-3 (Implicit Memory Learning) and E5b-4 (Hybrid Search) are internal services with no new REST endpoints — tested via unit tests only
E5b-5 (Memory & Skills Management UI) is frontend-only — no new backend endpoints
E5b-6 (E7 Skill Pack Validation) validates seed data and routing logic via integration tests — no new REST endpoints
Token variables like {{userToken}}, {{adminToken}} etc. are resolved at runtime from E2 auth test fixtures
All endpoints are registered under the /api/v1/ai prefix
Known code review issue: frontend sends retentionDays=0 for 'Keep forever' but backend validates min(1) — see E5b-5 ISSUE #1
